name: Basic Cream Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 cups vegetable; chopped into 1/2-inch pieces (cauliflower, asparagus, broccoli, or any other vegetable)
2 tbsp miso, light OR 2 vegetable bouillon cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring water to a boil in a kettle. Coarsely chop the onion and celery. Thinly slice the garlic.
2. In a soup pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion, garlic, and celery. Cook and stir for 1 minute.
3. Add the chopped vegetables. Stir and cook for another 1 minute.
4. Pour in boiling water and return to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium. Cover and simmer for 8 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and stir in miso or vegetable bouillon cubes. Continue cooking for 2 more minutes, until the miso is dissolved and vegetables are tender.
6. With a hand blender, puree some of the soup directly in the pot, or blend a small amount in a separate bowl with tahini, then return to the pot.
7. Pour the cooled soup into a bowl. Remove half of the broth from the soup and stir the remaining soup to cool it.
8. Place the remaining three-quarters of the soup into a blender and blend until smooth. Return this to the original soup pot.
9. Add the remaining one-quarter of unblended soup to a blender and pulse for 2-3 seconds, leaving it lumpy and textured. Pour back into the creamy portion.
10. Stir in the tahini mixture over medium heat, taking care not to boil. Add pepper if desired.
Variations:
If you don't have miso, substitute with 2 vegetable bouillon cubes or 5 cups of homemade stock instead of water.
Add dried thyme, basil, or marjoram when sautéing the vegetables for added flavor.
Add nutritional yeast to the soup pot when adding water.
Note: After adding tahini mixture, avoid boiling as it will cause thickening. Reheat gently and avoid overheating.
